subject: Sports
title: Lactic Acid: Friend, Not Foe
description: A quick, practical guide to Lactic Acid: Friend, Not Foe.

hook.text: For decades, coaches blamed lactic acid for muscle soreness after exercise. They were completely wrong.

buildup.text: During intense exercise, muscles produce lactate—a byproduct of anaerobic energy production.

discovery.text: Lactate is actually fuel, not waste. The heart and brain use it for energy during hard effort.

twist.text: Muscle soreness after exercise comes from microscopic muscle damage, not lactate buildup.

climax.text: Modern training now uses lactate threshold testing to find the perfect intensity for peak performance.

punchline.text: Lactic acid isn't the villain. It's fuel your body recycles.

quiz.question: What role does lactate actually play during exercise?
options: It serves as fuel for the heart and brain
